blocks.content: em3k supports three types of multimedia blocks to make your lessons engaging and visual.
Image Blocks: Display a single image with an optional caption. Set fluid to true for responsive sizing.

blocks.alt: Example image block with caption displayed below
blocks.caption: This is an example image block with a caption

blocks.content: Slideshow Blocks: Create an image carousel with multiple slides. Each slide can have its own caption.
Video Blocks: Embed videos from YouTube or other sources. The aspect ratio is maintained automatically for responsive design.
Store your lesson-specific images in the lesson's assets folder for portability.
Callout blocks draw attention to important information. Use variants like 'tip', 'warning', 'note', or 'danger' to set the visual tone:

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.content: Callout blocks are great for highlighting best practices and helpful hints.

blocks.title: ⚠️ Warning
blocks.content: Use warning callouts to alert learners about common mistakes or important prerequisites.
Table blocks display structured data using Bootstrap-styled HTML tables. Tables support striping, borders, and hover effects for better readability.
